steps.assistant_text: Trailing-nop / unfilled-jr-delay class. The -O2 body was already instruction-identical to the target (varargs decl `(char*,int,int,...)` homes all four arg regs at a 0x20 frame; `((int*)&a2)[-1]` forces the 3rd-arg memory reload `lw a2,36(sp)`). The residual was a SYMBOL-SIZE artifact: splat had split gl_func_0006EF08.s at 0x54 ending in a bare `jr ra` and carved the jr-ra delay nop into a separate _pad.s sidecar, so the baseline function symbol was 0x54 — but a C-compiled `jr ra` always owns its delay slot, so -O2 emits a 0x58 symbol. objdiff scored 95.65% on that 1-word symbol-boundary mismatch. Investigated -g3 (the prompt's unfilled-jr-delay hypothesis): REJECTED, -g3 reschedules the prologue store pair (sw ra/sw a0) and the epilogue (lw ra/addiu sp/move v0), diverging the body. Investigated REPLACE_FUNC_BODY donor splice: the splice keys on the function symbol size, so a donor's trailing alignment pad is outside the symbol and never spliced (skip). Resolved by correcting the splat boundary: merged the delay nop into gl_func_0006EF08.s (size 0x58) so the baseline symbol matches real -O2 codegen, then appended the genuine 1-word all-zero alignment pad at 0x6EF60 via SUFFIX_BYTES_FORCE (sanctioned all-zero data pad; FORCE since the function now ends in the natural jr-ra;nop epilogue) so gl_func_0006EF64 lands at +0x5C. _pad.s sidecar deleted. Verified against a true INCLUDE_ASM baseline (refresh-expected-baseline.py): 100.0 fuzzy + clean-build ROM byte-identical.

metadata.compiler_flags: -O2 -G 0 -mips2 -32 -non_shared -Xcpluscomm -Wab,-r4300_mul; SUFFIX_BYTES_FORCE one all-zero alignment-pad word; gl_func_0006EF08.s delay-nop merged (0x54->0x58)
metadata.verification: clean-build ROM byte-identical + objdiff fuzzy=100 vs INCLUDE_ASM baseline
